decks.notes: This Esper Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells and removal spells while utilizing powerful card draw and recursion options. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value from the graveyard. The deck aims to control the game through efficient use of its resources, using cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Wasteland to maintain dominance on the battlefield. Additionally, the deck utilizes key cards such as Ancestral Recall, Urza's Saga, and Demonic Tutor to ensure consistency and adaptability in different matchups.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's plays with lock pieces like Sphere of Resistance, Thorn of Amethyst, and Null Rod, while also generating mana advantage with Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb. It utilizes artifact creatures like Patchwork Automaton and Golos, Tireless Pilgrim to apply pressure early in the game. The deck also has access to powerful artifacts like Black Lotus and Karn, the Great Creator to further solidify its board presence. Overall, the strategy revolves around overwhelming the opponent with resilient threats and resource denial.

This deck aims to quickly establish a board presence with powerful artifact creatures and utility cards like Lodestone Golem and Tezzeret. By utilizing mana acceleration from Ancient Tomb and Mishra's Workshop, the deck can quickly cast threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Scrawling Crawler. With cards like Manifold Key and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to support its artifacts, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive artifacts. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Time Vault and Mystic Forge provides additional ways to close out games efficiently.

This UW Control vintage deck focuses on efficient disruption and resource management, utilizing cards like Force of Will, Mindbreak Trap, and Swords to Plowshares to control the game. The deck also includes powerful mana acceleration with Moxen and fast card draw with cards like Ancestral Recall and Treasure Cruise. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value and resilience in longer games. Overall, the deck aims to control the game, disrupt the opponent's strategy, and eventually win through card advantage and powerful threats.

This deck focuses on utilizing the Doomsday combo to win the game. The deck includes key cards like Ancestral Recall, Black Lotus, Doomsday, Force of Will, and Thassa's Oracle to quickly and efficiently assemble the combo. It also includes various card draw and disruption spells to help protect the combo and dig for the necessary pieces. The strategy revolves around setting up a Doomsday pile that will allow the player to win the game on the following turn, ensuring a swift victory in the Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this Dredge combo deck is to mill through your deck quickly using cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder to get key cards into your graveyard. By using Dredge creatures like Stinkweed Imp and Golgari Thug, you can quickly return creatures from your graveyard to play and overwhelm your opponent. Using disruptive cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation, along with protection like Chalice of the Void and Mental Misstep, you can ensure your combo goes off without a hitch. Overall, the deck aims to quickly fill your graveyard and swarm the board with creatures for a powerful win condition.

This deck utilizes the graveyard as a resource to quickly fill the board with powerful creatures like Hollow One, Prized Amalgam, and Ichorid. By using cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Golgari Thug to quickly fill the graveyard, the deck can easily bring back multiple creatures in one turn and overwhelm the opponent. Additionally, cards like Creeping Chill can drain the opponent's life while cards like Stinkweed Imp and Golgari Grave-Troll provide additional graveyard recursion. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect its combo pieces.

The deck focuses on using control elements like counterspells, removal, and card advantage to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain board control. It also utilizes powerful cards like Ancestral Recall and Black Lotus to gain an advantage in card advantage and mana acceleration. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional recursion and value. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and grind out a victory through attrition and resource management.

The primary strategy of this deck is to control the game by using Oath of Druids to put powerful creatures into play quickly. The deck includes a combination of disruptive cards like Null Rod and Thoughtseize, along with powerful card draw and manipulation with Brainstorm and Ponder. Show and Tell and Flash are used to cheat big threats onto the battlefield, while Atraxa, Grand Unifier provides additional control and pressure. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying powerful threats to win the game.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's plays with hate cards like Chancellor of the Annex, Void Mirror, and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Seasoned Dungeoneer and Clarion Conqueror. It also utilizes powerful mana acceleration with cards like Chrome Mox and Ancient Tomb to quickly deploy threats and lock down the opponent's options. The deck aims to control the game from the early turns and overwhelm the opponent with disruptive creatures and disruptive spells like Karakas and Vexing Bauble.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game in a single turn using Doomsday. It utilizes cards like Ancestral Recall, Black Lotus, and Brainstorm to draw cards and set up the Doomsday pile. Cards like Force of Will and Flusterstorm provide protection while cards like Gush, Gitaxian Probe, and Ponder help dig for combo pieces. Thassa's Oracle is the win condition once the Doomsday pile is set up, allowing the player to win the game on the spot. The deck also includes disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Mental Misstep to control the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing powerful control elements and card advantage to outlast opponents while maintaining control of the game. It includes a mix of powerful spells like Ancestral Recall, Force of Will, and Tinker, along with key artifacts like Time Vault and Bolas's Citadel. The deck also features tutors and card draw to assemble combos and answers efficiently. With a mix of countermagic, removal, and card advantage, the Esper Control archetype aims to control the pace of the game and outmaneuver opponents until it can secure a win condition.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly with cards like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith, allowing the player to play powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Coveted Jewel. The deck also includes control elements such as Force of Will and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent. The goal is to assemble the Coveted Jewel along with other artifacts to generate a significant advantage and win the game quickly. Time Vault and Time Walk provide extra turns to further solidify the win condition.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with artifact accelerants like the Moxen and Sol Ring, using Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb to power out powerful artifacts like Coveted Jewel and Time Vault. The deck also utilizes a classic combo of Time Vault and Voltaic Key to take extra turns. With cards like Trinisphere and Force of Will for disruption, and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator for tutoring and additional artifact synergy, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful artifact interactions.

The main objective of this deck is to establish a disruptive aggro strategy by utilizing powerful artifacts such as Sphere of Resistance, Null Rod, and Lodestone Golem to slow down the opponent while deploying threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Stonecoil Serpent to apply pressure and finish the game quickly. The deck also focuses on resource denial with cards like Strip Mine, Ghost Quarter, and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Additionally, the deck has a strong artifact synergy theme with cards like Mystic Forge and Karn, the Great Creator to generate card advantage and control the board.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through disruption and removal spells while using powerful card draw and tutor effects to find key cards. It utilizes a mix of counterspells, removal, and disruptive creatures to keep the opponent's game plan in check. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds additional value and recursion to the deck. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent, using its resources efficiently to stay ahead in the game.
